name: String-theoretic black hole microstate counting
summary: For extremal supersymmetric (BPS) black holes in 4D and 5D string compactifications, count the number of bound states of D-branes carrying the same charges. The logarithm of this microstate count exactly reproduces the Bekenstein-Hawking entropy A / (4 G_N) at leading order, showing that the entropy has an explicit microscopic origin in stringy degrees of freedom for this class of black holes.

addresses_puzzles.note: Provides a microscopic origin for BH entropy in the BPS case; demonstrates that the relevant degrees of freedom exist in string theory. The information paradox is reformulated rather than directly resolved — for non-extremal evaporating BHs, microscopic dynamics is harder.

introduces.note: Compactification choice, D-brane charges, string coupling, BPS charges. Heavily constrained by SUSY.

history: Strominger and Vafa (1996), 'Microscopic origin of the Bekenstein-Hawking entropy', Phys. Lett. B 379, 99, arXiv:hep-th/9601029. Generalised through 1996-2000 to broader BPS settings; remains the canonical microscopic explanation of BH entropy in supersymmetric string vacua.

references.citation: Strominger and Vafa, 'Microscopic origin of the Bekenstein-Hawking entropy', Phys. Lett. B 379, 99 (1996)
